title: 96-well screen prep checklist
description: Bench card for setting up the 96-well fluorescence screen.

entries.title: 2026-04-18: screen prep checklist
entries.content:
Bench card for setting up the 96-well fluorescence screen.

The night before:
- Pick 80 candidate colonies into 200 µL SD-Ura in deep-well plate
- Pick 8 WT colonies into the same plate (column 1)
- Pick 8 pDEMO-fluo+ positive control colonies (column 12)
- 30 °C, shaking 200 rpm, 16 to 18 h

Morning of:
1. Pre-warm SD-Ura + 2% galactose (induction media) to 30 °C
2. Spin deep-well plate 3000 g, 5 min
3. Wash pellets 1× with sterile water
4. Resuspend in 200 µL induction media
5. Transfer 50 µL to clear-bottom 96-well reader plate
6. Reader settings: 485/528 nm, every 15 min, 6 h, 30 °C

Double-check before starting the reader:
- [ ] Plate lid clean (no condensation = no scatter)
- [ ] Empty wells have water (corner evaporation correction)
- [ ] BioTek H1 calibrated this week (see note 6)

comments.author: mira
comments.text: Walk me through this one in our next 1:1 — I want to make sure the corner-evaporation control is set up correctly before the full screen runs. The water-only wells should be in the same column as your positive control, not opposite it.
